info.name: s-building — admin + user API
info.description:
Building CRUD with scoped permissions + Typesense-backed lists.

**Setup**
1. Set `{{baseUrl}}` to the stage you want to hit (default `https://dev.s-api.smartiqi.com`).
2. `POST /authn/auth/login` (out of scope for this collection) to get a JWT; paste it into `{{accessToken}}`.
3. Use `{{buildingId}}` to address a specific row.

**Audiences**
- `/building/admin/*` — superadmin / admin / manager audience. Returns 403 when the caller lacks the right permission for the target building.
- `/building/user/*` — consumer audience. Returns 404 (never 403) on anything hidden: out of scope, not active, missing row.

**Common error codes**
- `401` — missing or invalid `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>`.
- `400` — zod validation failed (e.g. `per_page > 100`, `supportedLanguages` missing the `primaryLanguage`, or `filter_by` contains `(`, `)`, `|` as a non-superadmin caller).
- `403` — admin audience: caller lacks the permission or scope. Specifically: create/archive/activate/delete require `building_superadmin` or scoped `building_admin`; manager is allowed for read/PATCH only.
- `404` — user audience on any hidden path; admin audience for a non-existent id.
- `409` — illegal status transition (`draft`→`archived` via `:archive` without first activating is a common example).
- `:archive` / `:activate` paths are AIP-136 custom actions — public URL is `POST /buildings/{id}:archive`; the module internally rewrites to `/buildings/{id}/_actions/archive` because Hono's router can't natively parse `:verb` syntax.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Karlín Tower",
  "description": "Office building in Karlín",
  "address": {
    "formatted": "Karlínské nám. 5, 186 00 Praha 8, Czech Republic",
    "streetAddress": "Karlínské nám. 5",
    "locality": "Praha",
    "countryCode": "CZ"
  },
  "areaSqm": 4200,
  "population": 350,
  "primaryLanguage": "en",
  "supportedLanguages": ["en"],
  "currency": "EUR",
  "timezone": "Europe/Prague"
}

item.name: User · List active buildings in caller's scope
User · Get active building (404 on hidden)

request.description: Returns 200 only when the building is active AND the caller has it in building_user scope (or is superadmin). Otherwise 404 — never 403.
